claim_short: 0903.3921 Argyros Haydon concluding problem asks whether every strictly singular operator on an L_infty space is weakly compact. 1103.6064 Aviles Cabello Sanchez Castillo Gonzalez Moreno records an L_infty space Omega in a twisted sum 0 -> C[0,1] -> Omega -> c_0 -> 0 with strictly singular quotient map q. Since C[0,1] contains c_0, composing q with an embedding c_0 -> C[0,1] -> Omega gives a strictly singular...
notes: registry_status=literature_implied_answer (negative answer to L_infty strictly singular weakly compact problem)
packet_title: Literature-Implied Answer: A Strictly Singular Non-Weakly-Compact Operator on an L_infty-Space
source_authors: Spiros A Argyros, Richard G Haydon
source_question: Source paper: S. A. Argyros and R. G. Haydon, *A hereditarily indecomposable L_infty-space that solves the scalar-plus-compact problem*, arXiv:0903.3921. Location: ConcRem.tex, concluding open problems, lines 280--293 in the parsed source. Question: if X is a \mathscr L_\infty-space, must every strictly singular operator on X be weakly compact?
source_title: A hereditarily indecomposable L_\infty-space that solves the scalar-plus-compact problem
